욥기 6:2.content: “Oh that my anguish were weighed, and all my calamity laid in the balances!

욥기 6:3.content: For now it would be heavier than the sand of the seas, therefore have my words been rash.

욥기 6:4.content: For the arrows of the Almighty are within me. My spirit drinks up their poison. The terrors of God set themselves in array against me.

욥기 6:5.content: Does the wild donkey bray when he has grass? Or does the ox low over his fodder?

욥기 6:6.content: Can that which has no flavor be eaten without salt? Or is there any taste in the white of an egg?

욥기 6:7.content: My soul refuses to touch them. They are as loathsome food to me.

욥기 6:8.content: “Oh that I might have my request, that God would grant the thing that I long for,

욥기 6:9.content: even that it would please God to crush me; that he would let loose his hand, and cut me off!

욥기 6:10.content: Let still be my consolation, yes, let me exult in pain that doesn’t spare, that I have not denied the words of the Holy One.

욥기 6:11.content: What is my strength, that I should wait? What is my end, that I should be patient?

욥기 6:12.content: Is my strength the strength of stones? Or is my flesh of bronze?

욥기 6:13.content: Isn’t it that I have no help in me, That wisdom is driven quite from me?

욥기 6:14.content: “To him who is ready to faint, kindness should be shown from his friend; even to him who forsakes the fear of the Almighty.

욥기 6:15.content: My brothers have dealt deceitfully as a brook, as the channel of brooks that pass away;

욥기 6:16.content: Which are black by reason of the ice, in which the snow hides itself.

욥기 6:17.content: In the dry season, they vanish. When it is hot, they are consumed out of their place.

욥기 6:18.content: The caravans that travel beside them turn away. They go up into the waste, and perish.

욥기 6:19.content: The caravans of Tema looked. The companies of Sheba waited for them.

욥기 6:20.content: They were distressed because they were confident. They came there, and were confounded.

욥기 6:21.content: For now you are nothing. You see a terror, and are afraid.

욥기 6:22.content: Did I say, ‘Give to me?’ or, ‘Offer a present for me from your substance?’

욥기 6:23.content: or, ‘Deliver me from the adversary’s hand?’ or, ‘Redeem me from the hand of the oppressors?’

욥기 6:24.content: “Teach me, and I will hold my peace. Cause me to understand my error.

욥기 6:25.content: How forcible are words of uprightness! But your reproof, what does it reprove?

욥기 6:26.content: Do you intend to reprove words, since the speeches of one who is desperate are as wind?

욥기 6:27.content: Yes, you would even cast lots for the fatherless, and make merchandise of your friend.

욥기 6:28.content: Now therefore be pleased to look at me, for surely I will not lie to your face.

욥기 6:29.content: Please return. Let there be no injustice. Yes, return again. My cause is righteous.

욥기 6:30.content: Is there injustice on my tongue? Can’t my taste discern mischievous things?
